topic: Run Gemma 4 Locally in 2026: 31B Dense Setup Guide with Ollama

key_points: Step-by-step Ollama install → pull → run workflow
Hardware match: 4B for laptops, 12B for mid-range GPUs, 27B/31B for high-end
Open WebUI setup for ChatGPT-like browser interface
Multimodal capability highlighted (text + image input)
Free tier focus — no API key, no token limits after download
All four Gemma 4 model sizes covered
Covers Windows, macOS, and Linux
Thinking mode activation using <|think|> token
Compares three local inference backends: Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM
Quantization options (Q4, Q8) and their VRAM trade-offs explained
vLLM recommended for production multi-user serving
llama.cpp for maximum CPU-friendly portability
VRAM benchmark table for all four model sizes
RTX 4060 Ti 16GB covers 31B at Q4 (18–20GB file)
Apple M-series with 24GB+ unified memory also viable
Includes token generation speed benchmarks per GPU tier
2026 guide with Ollama v0.20.0+ requirement noted
ollama pull gemma4:31b command and variant tags explained
Local REST API on port 11434 integration guide

stats.fact: Gemma 4 31B scores 89.2% on AIME 2026 math benchmark, up from Gemma 3's 20.8%
Gemma 4 31B scores 80.0% on LiveCodeBench v6 (coding), vs Gemma 3's 29.1%
Gemma 4 31B scores 84.3% on GPQA Diamond (science), vs Gemma 3's 42.4%
Gemma 4 31B ranked #3 on LM Arena leaderboard as of April 2026
Gemma 4 uses approximately 2.5x fewer tokens than rival models for the same task
31B Dense needs minimum 20GB RAM for 4-bit quantization, 34GB for 8-bit
Gemma 4 31B context window is 256K tokens (vs 128K for E2B/E4B variants)
Gemma 4 released April 2, 2026 under Apache 2.0 license — first Gemma with no usage restrictions
Ollama v0.20.0+ required for native Gemma 4 architecture support

angles: 31B Dense vs 26B MoE: Dense activates all parameters every token — better for accuracy-critical tasks; MoE activates only 4B params — faster throughput. Most local users want 31B for quality.
Hardware reality check: RTX 4060 Ti 16GB is the practical minimum for 31B Q4; M2/M3 Max/M4 Max with 32GB unified memory is the best consumer option.
Apache 2.0 license advantage: Unlike Llama 4 (MAU limits) or older Gemma models, Gemma 4 can be used commercially with zero restrictions — major unlock for indie devs.
Thinking mode activation: Adding <|think|> token in system prompt enables step-by-step reasoning — a unique feature not common in locally-hosted models.
Ollama as the easiest on-ramp: One-command install, pull, run — compared to llama.cpp (requires cmake build) or vLLM (Python environment setup). Best for beginners.
Open WebUI as ChatGPT replacement: Docker-based browser UI connects to local Ollama — creates a fully private, offline ChatGPT alternative.
Performance leap from Gemma 3: 89.2% vs 20.8% on AIME 2026 — nearly a 4x jump in math reasoning. Headline stat for the post.
Quantization guide: Q4_K_M balances quality vs size; Q8_0 is near full precision; full fp16 requires 60GB+ VRAM (cloud only).
